clusters.title: US Airman Rescued Amid Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz
clusters.summary: A missing U.S. airman has been successfully rescued as tensions escalate in the region, particularly concerning Iran's actions towards neighboring countries. In response to the situation, the U.S. government has issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning of severe consequences if the demand is not met. This incident highlights the ongoing geopolitical challenges in the area and the implications for U.S.-Iran relations.

clusters.title: Pope Leo XIV Calls for Peace in First Easter Mass
clusters.summary: Pope Leo XIV celebrated his first Easter Mass, emphasizing the need for peace and harmony amid global conflicts. In his Urbi et Orbi address, he refrained from naming specific conflicts, instead urging unity and compassion in a world facing turmoil. The message resonated with many as a call for hope during challenging times.

items.description: Pope Leo XIV addressed thousands of worshippers gathered in St Peter's Square on Easter Sunday in his first address as pontiff.

items.title: Pope Leo Celebrates First Easter Holiday as Pontiff

clusters.title: Trump Threatens Military Action Against Iran Over Hormuz Closure
clusters.summary: President Donald Trump has threatened to launch strikes on Iran's infrastructure, including power plants and bridges, if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by a specified deadline. This escalation comes after the rescue of a U.S. aviator, highlighting rising tensions in the region. The situation underscores the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz for global oil shipping and security.

clusters.summary: The astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission are currently on the third day of their journey, orbiting the far side of the Moon. They have expressed feelings of awe and humility as they witness the Moon from a new perspective, highlighting the significance of this historic mission in advancing lunar exploration. The crew is also managing their human needs while conducting their scientific objectives in space.

clusters.title: Federal Judge Blocks Trump's Race Data Collection from Colleges
clusters.summary: A federal judge in Boston has temporarily blocked the Trump administration's initiative to require public colleges in 17 states to submit data on race considerations in admissions. This move comes in response to the administration's efforts to ensure compliance with a recent Supreme Court ruling that ended affirmative action in college admissions. The ruling has implications for how colleges manage their admissions processes and address diversity.

items.title: Federal judge blocks Trump push to collect race-based admissions data
items.description: A federal judge in Boston temporarily blocked the Trump administration from forcing public colleges in 17 Democrat-led states to submit race data.

items.title: Judge Pauses Trump Demand for Student Race Data in 17 States
items.description: The Trump administration had said it would collect data from colleges to ensure compliance with a Supreme Court ruling ending affirmative action in admissions.
